7565909374636444959.text: You can do a green wall. But you have to provide a structure for the plants & pick the right plants. My green wall has brackets & wires that hold the twining plant away from the wall which lets the wall & plant breath. It also creates better conditions to cool the wall. English ivy is absolutely going to destroy a masonry wall no matter what you do. A house in my neighborhood with historic brick and lime sand mortar let the English Ivy grow for decades. The structural brick wall partially collapsed. Bricks were eaten away & the mortar was gone. Modern structures w/ Portland cement mortar are more durable but ivy gets into every crevice & can cause leaks, trim buckling, block gutters, & on & on. The Ivy League spends a LOT of money dealing with the ivy and repairing walls. They tend to use Boston ivy which is less destructive but still requires a good bit of maintenance.
